body:
## Summary

Fixes #2027.

Makes `gpt-5.6-sol` the default model for `--tool codex` and makes the thinking/effort levels predictable and consistent across both `codex` and `claude`. By default, both tools run the model as-is with **no thinking level enforced**.

## What changed (per requirement)

- **R1 — Default to GPT-5.6 Sol.** `defaultModels.codex` is now `gpt-5.6-sol`. `primaryModelNames.codex` leads with `gpt-5.6-sol`, and `gpt-5.5` is the first entry in `CODEX_DEFAULT_FALLBACK_CHAIN`, so environments whose local Codex catalog has not exposed Sol yet still resolve a working model.
- **R2 — All other models still supported.** The full Codex allowlist and fallback chain are preserved; no model IDs were removed. Regression tests assert the catalog still validates.
- **R3 — Predictable Codex levels.** `--think` maps 1:1 to Codex `model_reasoning_effort`: `off`→`none`, `low`/`medium`/`high`/`xhigh`/`ultra`/`max` identity. `xhigh` is natively supported (confirmed by `codex debug models`); `max` sits above `xhigh`; `ultra` is GPT-5.6 Sol's multi-agent mode. `--think ultra` is always paired with a `rollout_token_budget` cap (`500000` default, override via the new `--rollout-token-budget`). Budget-derived effort caps at `xhigh` so `max`/`ultra` require an explicit `--think`.
- **R4 — Align Claude.** Added the `ultra` level (ultracode-class): `thinkLevelToEffortLevel` clamps `ultra` to the highest supported effort (`max`, else `xhigh`, else `high`), `getThinkingLevelToTokens` maps `ultra` to the max token budget, and the thinking prompt emits `Ultrathink.`.
- **R5 — No thinking by default.** With no `--think`, `resolveCodexReasoningEffort` returns `reasoning_effort=none` (source `default`) and Claude adds no effort/budget overrides.
- **R6 — Case study.** Research data and analysis collected under [`docs/case-studies/issue-2027`](docs/case-studies/issue-2027/README.md), including the authoritative `codex debug models` output and GPT-5.6 Sol reasoning notes.

## Reproduction / verification

- `tests/test-codex-support.mjs` — default `gpt-5.6-sol`, primary/fallback ordering, runtime fallback to `gpt-5.5` when Sol is absent, and the identity reasoning mapping (`ultra` with `rolloutTokenBudget=500000`, overridable to `250000`; `max` with no budget; `xhigh` budget cap; `none` default/off).
- `tests/test-agent-commander-option.mjs` — Codex extra args for `xhigh`, `max`, and `ultra` (the latter appends `rollout_token_budget`), plus a custom `--rollout-token-budget` override.
- `tests/test-claude-think-prompt-gating.mjs` and `tests/test-opus-48-model-support.mjs` — the `ultra` level in Claude thinking-prompt gating and token/effort mapping.
- Docs sync/validation: `tests/test-docs-language-sync.mjs`, `tests/docs-validation.mjs`, `tests/test-docs-options-sync.mjs`.

All of the above pass locally (run with `CLAUDE_CODE_EFFORT_LEVEL`/`CLAUDE_EFFORT` unset, since the CI/dev harness leaks those env vars into unrelated Claude-effort tests).

## Notes

- The local `codex debug models` (`codex-cli 0.142.5`) does not yet list the GPT-5.6 preview models; hive-mind's registry already accepts them (added in #1992), so this change is the default flip plus the thinking-level work.
- A `minor` changeset is included for the next auto-release.

commits.messageBody:
Adding .gitkeep for PR creation (default mode).
This file will be removed when the task is complete.

Issue: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/2027

Issue #2027:
- Set gpt-5.6-sol as the default model for --tool codex (was gpt-5.5),
  keeping gpt-5.5 as the first runtime fallback when the local Codex
  catalog has not exposed Sol yet. All other Codex models stay supported.
- Map the shared --think levels 1:1 to Codex reasoning efforts
  (off=none, low/medium/high/xhigh/ultra/max as an identity mapping).
  xhigh is natively supported per 'codex debug models'; max sits above
  xhigh; ultra is GPT-5.6 Sol's multi-agent mode.
- Always pair --think ultra with a rollout_token_budget cap
  (CODEX_ULTRA_ROLLOUT_TOKEN_BUDGET=500000, override via
  --rollout-token-budget). Budget-derived effort caps at xhigh so max
  and ultra require an explicit --think.
- Align --tool claude by adding the ultra level (ultracode-class):
  thinkLevelToEffortLevel clamps ultra to the highest supported effort,
  getThinkingLevelToTokens maps ultra to the max token budget, and the
  thinking prompt emits 'Ultrathink.'.
- By default (no --think) both tools run the model as-is with no thinking
  enforced (Codex reasoning=none).
- Docs (en/zh/hi/ru), locales, tests, changeset, and a case study under
  docs/case-studies/issue-2027 updated accordingly.
commits.messageHeadline: feat(codex): default to gpt-5.6-sol and add predictable thinking levels

commits.messageBody:
Condense the Issue #2027 rollout_token_budget block (3-line comment +
multi-line if) to a single comment line + single-line if. Resolves the
lint (max-lines) and check-file-line-limits CI failures where the file
was 1501 lines (limit 1500).

title: Set GPT-5.6 Sol as default for `--tool codex` with predictable thinking levels
